title: Radio signal
description: One signal on the radio network (FR-STY-03 · GDD Part XIII · wireframe SCR-09). Static, content-as-data definition of a broadcast the survivor can pick up on a scavenged receiver. The engine (sim/radio.ts) is a generic interpreter — no per-signal branching — that DERIVES a signal's live/failing/faint/dead status from world + region + day state, so the network evolves as the world does. This file only supplies the identity (family, channel, reach, onset) and the message a status plays; the mechanical dials (thresholds, costs, noise) live in the engine. A signal's live condition is never stored — it is re-derived every read, so the save stays lossless.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing at this schema.

id.description: Stable content id of the form radio.<family>.<slug>, e.g. "radio.military.evac-stadium".

signalType.description: The GDD XIII signal family. Drives how the engine derives the signal's status from world state.

channel.description: The dial channel ("CH 7"). Omit for a signal with no honest channel — only the anomaly does this.

onsetDay.description: The in-game day this signal began transmitting — the aging reference ("a recorded loop, three days old"). A signal is not audible before its onset day.

regionId.description: The region whose fate drives a military/civilian/ham signal (its station goes dark when the region falls). Also the anchor for a `local` signal's reach. Omit for a placeless citywide signal (emergency, the number station).

reach.description: How far the signal carries on a portable receiver. `citywide` is audible anywhere; `local` only in or next to its `regionId` (a radio room, T52, would widen this).

lifespanDays.description: An emergency loop's shelf life: it falls silent this many days past onset even if the grid still holds (the old world running down). Emergency signals only.

onsetThreat.description: A signal that only APPEARS once world.globalThreat reaches this — "a new signal appears after a global event" (GDD XIII). Omit for a signal present from onsetDay.

anomaly.description: The reserved "signal that shouldn't exist" (SCR-09). Audible only under a rare engine gate (deep threat, at night, once per run) and breaks the screen's rules exactly once. At most one signal sets this.

messages.description: The prose a signal plays, keyed by derived status. The engine picks the variant; it never invents prose.

live.description: The message while the signal is transmitting normally (also the fallback for `failing`/`faint`).

failing.description: A military station holding but slipping — its region's threat is climbing. Falls back to `live` if absent.

dead.description: What remains once the signal goes silent — the honest DEAD-AIR row ("went quiet Day 5, mid-sentence"). A generic dead-air line is used if absent.

description.description: What the signal is and how it behaves (author/design reference).

notes.description: Author commentary (JSON has no comments, ADR-0002).
